question: Does Ascend/MindSpeed communication source contain a hidden post-router load-balancing scheduler, or does balance come from routing plus count-aware movement and grouped math?

findings.read: Expert load balance is created before dispatch. TorchTitan-NPU uses expert_bias for production-style expert choice and moe_force_load_balance for debug/exact round-robin ceiling.

TorchTitan-NPU permute exchanges num_tokens_per_expert, derives all-to-all splits, moves tokens/scores, then locally permutes received rows into expert-contiguous order with padding for grouped matmul.

MindSpeed npu_moe_token_permute wraps aclnnMoeTokenPermute. The wrapper sorts/gathers by existing expert indices, saves sorted_indices, and backward calls aclnnMoeTokenPermuteGrad to scatter gradients back. It does not alter token-to-expert assignment.
TorchTitan-NPU GMMFunction uses npu_grouped_matmul in forward and in both backward VJPs. This is the compact owner-rank math analogue AMD still lacks.
MORI dispatch/combine exposes capacity, buffer sizing, max receive token clamping, SDMA/AsyncLL, and local expert count atomics. It is transport scheduling, not a post-router load balancer.

amd_gap_mapping.load_balance: AMD has the same expert-bias and force-balance knobs, but short 0.02/0.01 coefficient runs did not collapse natural route skew. Forced balance remains a ceiling, not a production baseline.
amd_gap_mapping.layout: MORI dispatch moves tokens, but route-order probes show local rows are still fragmented even under exact balanced routing. AMD needs an explicit local expert-contiguous producer driven by saved forward route metadata.
amd_gap_mapping.math: Current MORI/AITER owner backward is adapter-heavy: gather compact rows, recompute gate/up, split W2 dgrad/wgrad, SwiGLU backward, W13 wgrads/xgrad, and dTopK. The Ascend-like target is one compact grouped owner VJP contract.
amd_gap_mapping.overlap: dX return overlap should come after the owner VJP tail is shortened; otherwise combine_dx mostly hides peer-arrival skew from imbalanced owner VJP.

next_rung: Build the AMD analogue of npu_moe_token_permute -> npu_grouped_matmul backward: saved-route expert-contiguous local compaction plus compact grouped owner VJP for dW13, dW2, dX, and dTopKWeight.
